name: Parque Natural Los Alcornocales
name_original: Parque Natural Los Alcornocales – circular desde Jimena de la Frontera
description: This difficult 67.8-mile (109.1 km) road cycling route in Parque Natural Los Alcornocales offers stunning cork oak forests and views.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Jimena De La Frontera traverse a landscape characterized by its position within the Los Alcornocales Natural Park. The region features rugged mountains, dense cork oak forests, and river valleys, offering varied terrain for cyclists. Elevation gains are common, with routes ranging from moderate to challenging climbs. The Hozgarganta River flows through picturesque valleys, adding to the natural features of the area. Best road cycling routes around Jimena De La Frontera The most popular road cycling route is Parque Natural Los Alcornocales, a 67.8 miles (109.1 km) trail that takes 5 hours 53 minutes to complete. This difficult route navigates through the expansive cork oak forests and mountainous terrain of the natural park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Castellar de la Frontera - circular from Jimena de la Frontera, a moderate 52.6 miles (84.6 km) path. This route offers a mix of ascents and descents through the rural surroundings of Jimena de la Frontera. Local road cyclists also love the Iron bridge loop from Jimena de la Frontera, a 38.6 miles (62.1 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 29 minutes. Road cycling around Jimena De La Frontera is defined by rugged mountains, cork oak forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging climbs with significant elevation gains. The routes in Jimena De La Frontera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from 9 reviews. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Jimena De La Frontera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Jimena de la Frontera?
A: The region offers varied terrain, characterized by its location within the Los Alcornocales Natural Park. You'll encounter rugged mountains, dense cork oak forests, and picturesque river valleys. Expect routes with significant elevation gains, ranging from moderate climbs to challenging ascents.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: While many routes feature significant elevation, there are options for less strenuous rides. For example, you can find flat cycle tracks along the railway towards Marchenilla, Castellar, and the coast. The komoot guide currently lists 6 moderate routes, though none are classified as easy, indicating most rides involve some level of challenge.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Jimena de la Frontera?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes around Jimena de la Frontera documented on komoot, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists.

Q: What are some of the more challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950800103/parque-natural-los-alcornocales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural Los Alcornocales&lt;/a&gt; cover over 109 km with more than 2,100 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5284742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ronda City Walls – Gaucín loop from San Pablo&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 2,300 meters of climbing over 104 km.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Jimena de la Frontera?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968507088/castellar-de-la-frontera-circular-from-jimena-de-la-frontera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellar de la Frontera - circular from Jimena de la Frontera&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a mix of ascents and descents through rural surroundings.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while road cycling in the region?
A: The routes often pass through the stunning Los Alcornocales Natural Park, known for its cork oak forests and diverse landscapes. You might also encounter the picturesque Hozgarganta River, which flows through valleys with oleander foliage. For specific viewpoints, consider routes that offer vistas of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5314207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Crestellina&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or castles accessible by road bike?
A: Absolutely. Jimena de la Frontera itself is home to the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Jimena de la Frontera&lt;/a&gt;, a Moorish-built castle offering panoramic views. Other routes might take you near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/670899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellar de la Frontera Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the charming village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5226462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gaucín&lt;/a&gt;, which also boasts a castle.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road biking in Jimena de la Frontera?
A: The road cycling routes in Jimena de la Frontera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 out of 5 stars. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, scenic views, and the immersive experience of riding through the natural park.

Q: Can I rent a road bike in Jimena de la Frontera?
A: Yes, bike hire is available in the area. For instance, tourist apartments at Hacienda Don Luis, near Jimena station, offer bike rentals, making it convenient to explore the region even if you don't bring your own bike.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Jimena de la Frontera?
A: The region's subtropical climate makes it suitable for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for longer rides. Summers can be hot, but early morning or late afternoon rides are still possible. Winters are mild, though some routes at higher elevations might be cooler.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through mountain passes?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain means several routes incorporate challenging passes. You might encounter passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4035367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de las Viñas&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5126765" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Mojón de la Víbora&lt;/a&gt;, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5126732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de las Asomadillas&lt;/a&gt;, offering rewarding climbs and descents with spectacular views.

Q: How long do road cycling routes typically take in this area?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and elevation. For example, a moderate route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38272038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iron bridge loop from Jimena de la Frontera&lt;/a&gt;, covering 62 km, can be completed in about 2 hours 30 minutes. More challenging routes, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950800103/parque-natural-los-alcornocales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural Los Alcornocales&lt;/a&gt;, can take nearly 6 hours.
tips: Nice little place and gateway to the national park with a breathtaking bike route in the north of Jimena.

faqs.question: Where does the road cycling route start in Jimena de la Frontera?
faqs.answer: This challenging circular road cycling route begins and ends in the charming town of Jimena de la Frontera. You'll find parking options within the town, making it a convenient starting point for your adventure into the park.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling in Parque Natural Los Alcornocales?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily on well-paved roads, offering a smooth ride for most parts. However, be prepared for significant elevation gains, as it includes extensive ascents. While generally good, some segments might be unpaved and difficult, potentially requiring you to push your bike. The rewarding descents are on roads reported to be in excellent condition.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as a "difficult" racebike tour, spanning over 109 kilometers with more than 2,190 meters of elevation gain. It requires a very good level of fitness and is best suited for experienced road cyclists.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll be immersed in the park's renowned cork oak forests, the largest in the Iberian Peninsula, alongside diverse landscapes of lush valleys and pine-covered hills. Notable points include the historic Jimena de la Frontera Castle and several mountain passes like Puerto de las Asomadillas, Puerto de Galiz, and Puerto del Mojón de la Víbora.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints along the cycling route?
faqs.answer: Yes, from elevated points around Jimena de la Frontera, especially near its hilltop castle, you can enjoy extensive views that, on a clear day, may even stretch beyond Gibraltar to the African coast. The mountain passes also offer panoramic vistas of the park's stunning nature.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to road cycle in Parque Natural Los Alcornocales?
faqs.answer: Given the significant elevation and potential for heat, spring and autumn are generally the best times for road cycling. The subtropical climate means coastal fog can occur, which might offer some relief in warmer months, but avoiding the peak heat of summer is advisable for this challenging route.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this road cycling route overlap with other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the [CIMA ML06] Puerto de los Pinos - Llano de las Cruces, [CIMA CD03] Gáliz * Jimena de la Frontera, Via Serrana, and Andalucía (Province of Cádiz).

faqs.question: Is Parque Natural Los Alcornocales dog-friendly for cyclists?
faqs.answer: While the park generally welcomes visitors, this specific road cycling route is long and challenging. Bringing dogs on such a demanding road ride is not typically recommended due to the distance, elevation, and potential traffic. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are well-conditioned and consider shorter, less strenuous routes within the park.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for Parque Natural Los Alcornocales?
faqs.answer: Generally, there is no entrance fee to access Parque Natural Los Alcornocales for activities like cycling on public roads. However, certain restricted areas or specific activities might require permits. For this road cycling route, you typically won't need a special permit, but it's always good practice to check the official park regulations for any updates before your visit.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while cycling?
faqs.answer: The park is known for its exceptional biodiversity. While cycling, you might spot various raptors such as golden eagles and griffon vultures soaring above. The cork oak forests and diverse landscapes are also home to red deer and wild boar, though they are often more elusive.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or water along the route?
faqs.answer: This route includes "away-from-it-all" sections with minimal traffic, meaning amenities can be sparse between villages. It's highly recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks for the entire 109-kilometer journey. Jimena de la Frontera, your starting point, offers cafes and pubs where you can refuel before and after your ride.

waypoints.name: Puerto del Mojón de la Víbora
